name: Secrets Baseline
description: Comprehensive secret and credential hygiene: requires admin approval when secrets are detected entering prompts and tool calls, redacts secrets in tool output, requires admin approval before reading well-known credential files, and catches hardcoded AWS access key patterns. Uses detection-based rules with fail-mode open to minimise false-positive disruption while still surfacing issues.

rules.name: Require access request when secrets are detected in user prompts
rules.description: Scans user prompts for API keys, tokens, passwords, and other credentials using the built-in gitleaks ruleset. Requires admin approval before credentials are submitted to AI context.

rules.name: Require access request when secrets are detected in tool call inputs
rules.description: Scans the inputs to every tool call for credentials before they are executed. Requires admin approval before secrets are written to files, run in commands, or sent to MCP servers.

rules.name: Redact secrets found in tool output
rules.description: Scans tool output for credentials after execution and redacts them before they reach the AI context. Does not block — allows execution to proceed with sanitised results.

rules.name: Require access request to read well-known credential files
rules.description: Reading .aws/credentials, SSH private keys, .env files, and /etc/shadow exposes secrets to AI context. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for hardcoded AWS access key patterns in prompts
rules.description: Detects the AKIA/ASIA/AROA AWS key ID pattern in user prompts. These 20-character keys starting with AKIA are always credentials. Requires admin approval before they proceed into AI context.

rules.name: Warn on commands that dump environment variables
rules.description: printenv and bare env commands dump all environment variables, which may include secrets. Logs a warning without blocking.
